Title: Rewiring of the Fruit Metabolome in Tomato Breeding

Genes.Variety: Multiple accessions (610 tomato accessions panel); enzyme assay in E. coli BL-21
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: UDP-glycosyltransferase that converts tomatidine into tomatidine-galactose
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Steroidal glycoalkaloids (SGAs), specifically tomatidine-galactose and related SGAs including SlFM1885
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Nonsense mutation (premature stop codon) significantly reduced content of SlFM1885; pyramiding SW29 and SW246 loci reduces three major SGAs to 20.9% of high-SGA genotype levels
Genes.Metabolic_Step_Type: Terminal_Step (glycosylation of tomatidine during fruit ripening)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Glycosylation of tomatidine contributes to SGA accumulation in ripe fruit; loss-of-function (nonsense mutation) reduces downstream SGA levels
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: A nonsense mutation (SNP ch10:64501127) producing a premature stop codon in Solyc10g085230 significantly reduces SGA content (e.g., SlFM1885) in ripe fruit, contributing to reduced anti-nutritional compound levels during domestication/improvement.
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Part of a newly identified co-expression gene cluster on chr 10 predominantly expressed during ripening, distinct from the GAME1 cluster expressed in immature fruits
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: GAME9->regulates->SGA pathway genes (upstream regulator); Solyc10g085230 is within the same SGA biosynthetic network
Genes.Developmental_Stage: Ripening stage (predominantly expressed during ripening, break + 10 days)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly expressed during fruit ripening (stages S5-S6: break and break+10 days); part of a chr 10 gene cluster with distinct ripening-stage expression pattern
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Natural variation panel (442 accessions); in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein expressed in E. coli BL-21; S. pennellii introgression line population (prior validation via VIGS)
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay (recombinant protein expressed in E. coli converted tomatidine to tomatidine-galactose); mGWAS association; nonsense mutation association with SGA content reduction; prior VIGS validation (Alseekh et al., 2015)
Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (26.3M SNPs); RNA-seq (transcriptome of 399 accessions, PRJNA396272); LC-MS/MS metabolomics (980 metabolites in 442 accessions)
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: ch10:64501127 (introduces premature stop codon in exon)
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Truncated protein allele (premature stop codon) associated with reduced SGA content (favorable for edibility)
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: mGWAS combined with domestication sweep analysis; prior identification in S. pennellii introgression line population (Alseekh et al., 2015)
Genes.Genetic_Population: Diversity panel of 442 red-fruited tomato accessions (31 PIM, 124 CER, 287 BIG)
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker-assisted selection: pyramiding low-SGA alleles at SW29 (GAME9) and SW246 (Solyc10g085230) reduces three major SGAs to 20.9%, mitigating anti-nutritional effects when using wild germplasm in breeding.
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g085230 encodes a UDP-glycosyltransferase that converts tomatidine to tomatidine-galactose, validated by in vitro enzyme assay. 2. A nonsense mutation (ch10:64501127) introducing a premature stop codon significantly reduces SGA content (e.g., SlFM1885) in ripe fruit. 3. The gene is part of a newly identified co-expression cluster on chr 10 (including acetyltransferase, cytochrome P450, acyl-CoA dehydrogenase, and 7 UGT genes) predominantly expressed during ripening. 4. Located within domestication/improvement sweep SW246, contributing to reduced anti-nutritional SGA levels during tomato breeding history.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This gene cluster on chr 10 has a markedly different expression pattern compared to the previously known GAME1 cluster (immature fruit). The chr 10 cluster functions during ripening to detoxify ripe fruits. Prior identification and VIGS validation by Alseekh et al. (2015) confirmed its role as causal for tomatidine-hexose.

Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Transcription factor regulating SGA biosynthesis and upstream isoprenoid (mevalonate) pathway
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Steroidal glycoalkaloids (SGAs; 8 SGAs associated with the GAME9 CDS variant)
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Low-SGA allele frequency increased from 0% (PIM) to 26.3% (CER) to 57.3% (BIG); combined with SW246 allele, reduces three major SGAs to 20.9%
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative content (LC-MS/MS, ripe fruit pericarp); allele frequency across populations
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: GAME9 regulates expression of SGA biosynthetic genes; the low-SGA allele (V→A non-synonymous mutation) reduces flux through SGA pathway
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: A non-synonymous SNP (V-A, ch01:84029382) in the CDS of GAME9 is associated with content alteration of 8 SGAs. The low-SGA allele was progressively selected during domestication (0% PIM → 26.3% CER → 57.3% BIG), indicating GAME9 played an important role in SGA domestication.
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional regulation of SGA biosynthetic pathway genes and upstream isoprenoid pathway (mevalonate pathway)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Natural variation panel (442 accessions); allele frequency analysis across PIM, CER, BIG groups
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association (SNP ch01:84029382 associated with 8 SGAs); allele frequency shift analysis across domestication/improvement groups; prior functional characterization (Cárdenas et al., 2016)
Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing; RNA-seq transcriptome; LC-MS/MS metabolomics
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Alanine allele (A) at position ch01:84029382 associated with lower SGA content (favorable for edibility)
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: mGWAS combined with domestication sweep analysis (SW29); prior identification (Cárdenas et al., 2016; Itkin et al., 2013)
Genes.Genetic_Population: Diversity panel of 442 red-fruited tomato accessions (31 PIM, 124 CER, 287 BIG)
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker-assisted selection: the low-SGA allele at GAME9 (SW29) combined with low-SGA allele at SW246 (Solyc10g085230) reduces three major SGAs to 20.9%, enabling use of wild germplasm while mitigating anti-nutritional effects.
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME9, located in domestication sweep SW29 on chr 1, is a transcription factor regulating SGA biosynthesis. 2. A non-synonymous SNP (V→A, ch01:84029382) in GAME9 CDS is associated with altered content of 8 SGAs. 3. The low-SGA allele frequency progressively increased during domestication/improvement (0% PIM → 26.3% CER → 57.3% BIG). 4. GAME9 is highly likely to have played an important role in SGA domestication.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GAME9 function was previously characterized (Cárdenas et al., 2016; Itkin et al., 2013). In this study, the allele frequency shift provides population-level evidence of selection. GAME9 regulates both SGA biosynthesis structural genes and upstream mevalonate pathway.

Genes.Variety: Moneymaker (for CRISPR/Cas9 knockout); diverse panel including 44 pink BIG and 191 red BIG lines
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Key regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis pathway; also directly or indirectly influences polyamine, SGA, polyphenol, and primary metabolite content
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoids (naringenin chalcone and other flavonoids); also affects steroidal glycoalkaloids, polyamines, polyphenols, and primary metabolites
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: 122 metabolites significantly different between pink (SlMYB12 non-functional) and red (SlMYB12 functional) BIG lines (p < 10⁻³); 152 metabolites altered in peel of CRISPR knockout mutants (p < 0.05); major flavonoids decreased
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative content (LC-MS/MS, fruit pericarp and peel)
Genes.Pathway_Name: Flavonoid biosynthesis; also affects SGA biosynthesis, polyamine biosynthesis, polyphenol metabolism
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: SlMYB12 activates flavonoid biosynthesis pathway; knockout eliminates flavonoid accumulation in peel and unexpectedly modifies 100+ other metabolites across multiple pathways
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of SlMYB12 in cv. Moneymaker produced pink fruit with colorless peel due to absence of flavonoids. 152 metabolites were significantly altered in peel of all four independent knockout lines, including 18 flavonoids (decreased), 16 SGAs, 12 polyamines, 5 polyphenols, and 11 primary metabolites.
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Pink fruit phenotype (colorless peel); 869 genes differentially expressed in peel (658 up, 211 down); potential influence on taste/consumer preference
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation of flavonoid pathway genes and other downstream targets; 18 of 44 genes reported as direct AtMYB12 targets (via ChIP) were also eQTL of SlMYB12
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: SlMYB12->activates->flavonoid biosynthesis genes; SlMYB12->regulates->Solyc05g041860 (N-acetyltransferase, polyamine biosynthesis); SlMYB12->regulates->Solyc04g063210 (caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase, polyamine biosynthesis); SNPy associated with 56 metabolites (mGWAS) and 69 genes (eQTL)
Genes.Developmental_Stage: Turning stage (predominantly expressed); orange stage (sampled for transcriptome)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly expressed in peel at the turning stage of fruit ripening
Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines (4 independent homozygous mutants M1-M4 in cv. Moneymaker with two sgRNAs); natural pink (n=44) vs red (n=191) BIG accessions
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout (2 sgRNAs, 4 independent homozygous lines); metabolite profiling of mutant vs wild-type peel; transcriptome analysis (RNA-seq) of mutant vs wild-type peel; mGWAS and eQTL analysis
Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (PRJNA396272); whole-genome resequencing; LC-MS/MS metabolomics (980 metabolites)
Genes.Key_Variant_Type: SNP; nonsense mutations; cis-acting element deletion in promoter
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: SNPy (signature SNP of SlMYB12); promoter cis-element deletion; multiple nonsense mutations in coding sequence
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Functional SlMYB12 allele (red fruit) associated with higher flavonoid content; non-functional alleles (pink fruit) lack flavonoids but have altered profiles of 100+ other metabolites
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified (Adato et al., 2009; Ballester et al., 2010; Lin et al., 2014); validated here by CRISPR/Cas9 knockout and multi-omics integration
Genes.Genetic_Population: Diversity panel of 442 red-fruited tomato accessions; segregation among pink vs red BIG accessions
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: SlMYB12 is a major hub gene of tomato fruit metabolism. Selection for pink fruit (non-functional SlMYB12) for consumer preference inadvertently modifies 100+ metabolites beyond flavonoids, with potential unexpected impacts on nutritional quality, flavor, and other fruit quality traits. Breeding should consider these pleiotropic metabolic effects.
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 is a key transcriptional regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis; its inactivation causes pink fruit phenotype. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout altered 152 metabolites in fruit peel, including 18 flavonoids, 16 SGAs, 12 polyamines, 5 polyphenols, and 11 primary metabolites. 3. mGWAS identified 56 metabolites and eQTL identified 69 genes associated with the SlMYB12 signature SNP. 4. 18 of 44 known direct AtMYB12 targets (ChIP-validated) overlapped with SlMYB12 eQTL genes. 5. SlMYB12 is a major metabolic hub gene; selection for pink tomatoes has far-reaching and unexpected metabolic consequences beyond flavonoid loss.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The 122 metabolites differing between pink and red accessions include metabolites in pericarp tissue (not just peel), suggesting broader metabolic influence. Two polyamine biosynthesis genes (Solyc05g041860, N-acetyltransferase; Solyc04g063210, caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase) were identified via mGWAS for changed polyamines and their expression was also altered in knockout mutants. A causal relationship between SlMYB12 and non-flavonoid metabolites awaits further experimentation.
